April 2018

April 2018

  • Saturday Morning Session

    • Saturday Morning Session

    • Henry B. Eyring

      Solemn Assembly

      In a solemn assembly, President Eyring presents the names of the First Presidency and Quorum of the Twelve Apostles for sustaining votes.

    • M. Russell Ballard

      Precious Gifts from God

      President Ballard teaches that exercising faith in Jesus Christ and recognizing the precious gifts from God in our lives brings joy to ourselves and others.

    • Brian K. Taylor

      Am I a Child of God?

      Elder Taylor teaches how we can be strengthened through understanding our divine identity as children of God.

    • Larry J. Echo Hawk

      Even as Christ Forgives You, So Also Do Ye

      Elder Echo Hawk teaches the principle of forgiveness with the example of his family forgiving a young man following an accident that took his brother’s life.

    • Gary E. Stevenson

      The Heart of a Prophet

      Elder Stevenson explains the divine process of the calling and sustaining of a living prophet on the earth.

    • Lynn G. Robbins

      Until Seventy Times Seven

      Elder Robbins teaches that sins and mistakes are part of life and that through sincere repentance, we can continually access God’s forgiveness and help.

    • Neil L. Andersen

      The Prophet of God

      Elder Andersen teaches that there is safety and peace in following the prophet, whose most important role is to point the way to the Savior.

  • Saturday Afternoon Session

    • Saturday Afternoon Session

    • Dallin H. Oaks

      The Sustaining of Church Officers

      President Oaks presents the General Officers for a sustaining vote.

    • Kevin R. Jergensen

      Church Auditing Department Report, 2017

      Kevin R. Jergensen, managing director of the Church Auditing Department, presents the auditing report for 2017.

    • David A. Bednar

      Meek and Lowly of Heart

      Sharing examples of meekness from the scriptures and the lives of modern-day prophets, Elder Bednar teaches that this Christlike attribute can be developed.

    • Taylor G. Godoy

      One More Day

      Elder Godoy teaches about the blessings that come through sacrifice.

    • Bonnie L. Oscarson

      Young Women in the Work

      Sister Oscarson outlines ways the young women of the Church can contribute to their wards and branches.

    • Taniela B. Wakolo

      Saving Ordinances Will Bring Us Marvelous Light

      Elder Wakolo discusses the role of ordinances, covenants, and Jesus Christ‘s Atonement in receiving the power of godliness in our lives.

    • Devin G. Durrant

      Teaching in the Home—a Joyful and Sacred Responsibility

      Brother Durrant talks about the sacred parental responsibility of teaching children and describes different kinds of teaching opportunities.

    • Dale G. Renlund

      Family History and Temple Work: Sealing and Healing

      Elder Renlund teaches that when we do family history and temple work for our ancestors, God bestows blessings on both sides of the veil.

  • General Priesthood Session

    • General Priesthood Session

    • Douglas D. Holmes

      What Every Aaronic Priesthood Holder Needs to Understand

      Brother Holmes teaches that Aaronic Priesthood holders have an important role in helping others to receive the Savior’s atoning power in their lives.

    • Russell M. Nelson

      Introductory Remarks

      President Nelson announces that high priests and elders, at the ward level, will be combined into one elders quorum.

    • D. Todd Christofferson

      The Elders Quorum

      Elder Christofferson explains the purposes for combining elders and high priests into one elders quorum at the ward level and gives details about this change.

    • Ronald A. Rasband

      Behold! A Royal Army

      Elder Rasband shares clarifying details about changes to the organization of Melchizedek Priesthood quorums and discusses the blessings of these changes.

    • Henry B. Eyring

      Inspired Ministering

      President Eyring teaches that ministering to others in the Lord’s way requires feeling love for each other and following the promptings of the Spirit.

    • Dallin H. Oaks

      The Powers of the Priesthood

      President Oaks shares principles governing the exercise of the Melchizedek Priesthood in the Church and in the home.

    • Russell M. Nelson

      Ministering with the Power and Authority of God

      President Nelson teaches that we should minister to one another in Christ’s name, with His power and authority, and with His loving kindness.

  • Sunday Morning Session

    • Sunday Morning Session

    • Larry Y. Wilson

      Take the Holy Spirit as Your Guide

      Elder Wilson teaches principles that lead to receiving personal revelation from the Lord through the Holy Ghost.

    • Reyna I. Aburto

      With One Accord

      Sister Aburto teaches that to reach our divine destiny we need to follow Christ’s example of unity with His Father and be more unified with Them and each other.

    • Massimo De Feo

      Pure Love: The True Sign of Every True Disciple of Jesus Christ

      Elder De Feo teaches that the gospel is centered on pure love and that our discipleship is an expression of our love for God and one another.

    • Claudio D. Zivic

      He That Shall Endure unto the End, the Same Shall Be Saved

      Elder Zivic teaches the importance of enduring to the end in faith, repentance, and obedience.

    • Henry B. Eyring

      His Spirit to Be with You

      President Eyring teaches about receiving the Holy Ghost, using New Testament scriptures and Joseph Smith as examples.

    • Dallin H. Oaks

      Small and Simple Things

      President Oaks reminds us that small and simple things, including our small, daily positive or negative choices, over time bring about great things.

    • Russell M. Nelson

      Revelation for the Church, Revelation for Our Lives

      President Nelson testifies that the Lord leads the Church through revelation and that personal revelation is available to everyone.

  • Sunday Afternoon Session

    • Sunday Afternoon Session

    • Gerrit W. Gong

      Christ the Lord Is Risen Today

      Elder Gong shares his powerful witness of the resurrected Savior, Jesus Christ, and of His modern-day prophets.

    • Ulisses Soares

      Prophets Speak by the Power of the Holy Spirit

      Elder Soares testifies that prophets are led by the Holy Spirit and teach of Jesus Christ.

    • Russell M. Nelson

      Ministering

      President Nelson introduces a newer, holier approach to caring for and ministering to others.

    • Jeffrey R. Holland

      “Be With and Strengthen Them”

      Elder Holland teaches that we should be motivated by the pure love of Christ as we become more committed to caring for and ministering to others.

    • Jean B. Bingham

      Ministering as the Savior Does

      Sister Bingham teaches about ministering to each other and gives examples of how we can follow the Savior’s example of loving service through simple acts.

    • Dieter F. Uchtdorf

      Behold the Man!

      Elder Uchtdorf testifies that the greatest day in history was when the Savior overcame sin and death. We can find joy and peace as we see Him for who He is.

    • Gérald Caussé

      It Is All about People

      Bishop Caussé teaches that the Church’s strength comes from the daily actions of millions of Christ’s disciples who strive each day to follow His example.

    • Quentin L. Cook

      Prepare to Meet God

      Elder Cook teaches that we prepare to meet God by fulfilling divinely given responsibilities in missionary work, temple work, and strengthening Church members.

    • Russell M. Nelson

      Let Us All Press On

      President Nelson encourages us to study the messages of this historic general conference. He announces the construction of seven new temples.
